steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must logically connect two clauses separated by a semicolon. The first clause says Chaucer didn't write in Modern English. The second clause gives the correction: he wrote in Middle English. The word However at the start of the sentence already signals a contrast with the previous sentence. The blank after the semicolon must continue that corrective logic — introducing an alternative rather than a consequence, similarity, or sequence.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'as a result,': signals cause-and-effect; the second clause is not a consequence of the first. ✗
  • Choice B — 'similarly,': signals similarity; the two clauses contrast, not compare. ✗
  • Choice C — 'rather,': signals correction or substitution ("not X, but rather Y"), which fits: not Modern English, but rather Middle English. ✓
  • Choice D — 'finally,': signals the last item in a sequence; there is no sequence here. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
